summary: Introduces the preterite tense (dot in the past) for -ar verbs: -é for I, -ó for he/she/it. Teaches the passive se structure in the preterite (se envió = it was sent), introduces todavía (still/yet) and olvidar (to forget).

teachings.conceptName: Preterite tense for -ar verbs (I and he/she)
teachings.summary: The preterite (dot/point in the past) for -ar verbs: remove -ar, add -é for I, -ó for he/she/it/usted. Both endings are accented. Note the I form uses -é (not -o), which is opposite to the present where I = -o.
teachings.rule: For -ar verbs: I = -é (hablé), he/she/it/usted = -ó (habló). Accented endings. Careful: I spoke = hablé (not habló). Present hablo (stress on HA-) vs preterite habló (stress on -BLÓ).

examples.english: I didn't send it to you yet

teachings.notes: The preterite is more messy/random than other tenses. The accent is critical: hablo (I speak, present) vs habló (he spoke, preterite).

teachings.summary: The reflexive se works in the preterite just like other tenses: se envió = it sent itself = it was sent. Don't add lo when using se - the se already completes the sentence.
teachings.rule: Se + preterite verb = passive meaning. Don't add lo/la with se.
teachings.notes: Resist the urge to say *se lo envió when you mean 'it was sent'. Se already completes the verb.

vocabulary.derivation: noun from olvidar; common in tango lyrics
